title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how agents often falsely claim successful web searches when blocked, hitting CAPTCHAs, or falling back to training data. The session compares blocked versus real web access on LinkedIn, Instagram, Amazon, and TikTok to show how genuine web connectivity reduces hallucinations and improves citation reliability.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data examines a common failure mode in AI agents: claiming to have searched the web while actually returning answers blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or stale training data. The talk argues this silent failure undermines reliability because agents present confident responses without genuine real-time access. The fix presented is real web access through MCP protocols paired with proper JavaScript rendering and anti-bot handling, which improves citation accuracy and reduces hallucinations.
